Meta.title: HeyHeyDive
Meta.description: Diving trips and dual-system certification courses (AIDA / PADI) at Taiwan's four signature dive sites: Liuqiu, Ludao, Lanyu, Kenting.

Footer.description: Taiwan's dive-travel brand — guiding you into the world beneath the surface.

hero.headline: Ludao Underwater Hot-spring Night Dive
hero.subtitle: Where geothermal currents meet the night sea.

tagline.main: The breath is where diving begins.

ludao.name: Ludao
ludao.desc: Underwater hot springs, twin-peak rocks, and steady visibility — Taiwan's geology, submerged.

lanyu.name: Lanyu
lanyu.desc: Kuroshio upwelling, blue caves, and pelagic encounters — the wild edge of Taiwan.

liuqiu.name: Liuqiu
liuqiu.desc: Year-round diving and turtle sightings every dive — Taiwan's entry point to the underwater world.

courses.subtitle: Choose your breathing style.

shared.coursesDesc: Begin your diving journey on this island — freediving or scuba, both certifications run year-round.

ludao.name: Ludao
ludao.intro: A volcanic island on Taiwan's east coast and one of only three undersea hot-spring sites on Earth. Steady currents, high visibility, varied topography.

narr1.title: An undersea hot spring — one of three on Earth
narr1.body: Sulphurous geothermal water meets cool sea water near Zhaori, producing thermal striations and clouds of orange shrimp on night dives.

narr2.title: Twin Peaks, Gate Rock, Da-baisha
narr2.body: Volcanic walls, swim-throughs, and tunnels — progression-ready dives once your buoyancy is dialled in.

narr3.title: Damselfish, turtles, and the occasional hammerhead
narr3.body: Pelagics ride the Kuroshio's edge. Mantas and hammerhead schools are seasonal but real.

lanyu.name: Lanyu
lanyu.intro: Home of the Tao people. Kuroshio upwelling fuels a wild marine ecosystem — blue caves, vertical walls, and pelagic giants.

narr1.title: The Kuroshio's richest reach
narr1.body: The main current sweeps the island, carrying tuna, mahi-mahi, and mantas in its push. Drift dives are the norm here.

narr2.title: Blue caves and swim-throughs
narr2.body: Maiden Rock, Twin Lions, and the Blue Cave — light columns through cobalt water. A photographer's pilgrimage.

narr3.title: Diving in the Tao homeland
narr3.body: Before you arrive, learn the flying-fish season, the meaning of the canoes, and the island's taboos. Respect is the first dive.

liuqiu.name: Liuqiu
liuqiu.intro: Taiwan's only coral-reef island. Steady warm water, gentle topography, and near-guaranteed turtle sightings year-round.

narr1.title: Turtle sightings: 99%
narr1.body: Coral and seagrass beds wrap the island — habitat for green sea turtles. Three minutes underwater and they're already with you.

narr2.title: The year-round entry island
narr2.body: Stable conditions, shallow average depth, low current — the first choice for try-dives and Open Water certification.

narr3.title: Fluorescent night at Shanfu
narr3.body: Strawberry conch, nudibranchs, fluorescing coral — Liuqiu, but only after dark.

Calendar.title: Trip Sign-up
Calendar.description: Pick a destination and a date. Book your next underwater chapter.

ludao-4d3n.title: Ludao 4D3N — Hot-spring dive trip
ludao-4d3n.subtitle: Four days, three nights, eight tanks, six sites — and the only undersea hot spring in Asia.

day1.title: Day 1 — Arrival & check-in dives
day1.body:
Meet in Taitung → ferry to Ludao → check into the guesthouse
Tank 1 — Da-baisha (orientation + reef walk)
Tank 2 — Shilang (coral reef)
Dinner at a local seafood spot

day2.title: Day 2 — Going deeper, then the hot spring
day2.body:
Tank 3 — Da Xianggu
Tank 4 — Twin Peaks
Afternoon free / Zhaori onsen
Night dive — Shilang

day3.title: Day 3 — Undersea hot spring, island loop
day3.body:
Tank 5 — Zhaori undersea hot-spring night dive
Tank 6 — Gate Rock
Afternoon island loop / sightseeing

day4.title: Day 4 — Farewell dives, return to Taitung
day4.body:
Tank 7 — Youziwu
Tank 8 — Shilang (farewell)
Lunch then ferry back to Taitung

audience.p1: This Ludao trip is built for Open Water (OW) certified divers and above. Whether you just got your card and want experience, or you're a seasoned diver looking for Taiwan's most distinctive sites, the itinerary has the range.
audience.p2: Full gear and an instructor are with you on every dive. From geothermal water columns to the giant mushroom coral, every tank earns its own memory.

instructor.name: Karina
instructor.bio: Underwater photographer and reef-ecology nerd. Her tank-side trivia is half the trip.

liuqiu-2d1n.title: Liuqiu 2D1N — Weekend turtle escape
liuqiu-2d1n.subtitle: A weekend you can actually make. Turtles on every tank, near-guaranteed.

day1.title: Day 1 — Arrival
day1.body:
Meet in Donggang → ferry to Liuqiu
Tank 1 — Shanfu (turtle hunt)
Tank 2 — Vase Rock
Dinner + island walk

day2.title: Day 2 — Last tank + island loop
day2.body:
Tank 3 — Meiren Cave
Free time / island loop
Afternoon ferry back to Donggang

audience.p1: Liuqiu's gentle terrain and stable conditions make it the best entry-level dive in Taiwan. Turtle sightings are essentially routine — most divers swim with a green turtle within minutes.
audience.p2: Full gear and an instructor support you the whole weekend, from first fin-on to your first turtle photo.

instructor.name: Daniel
instructor.creds: PADI MSDT · 8 years on Liuqiu · 300+ turtle encounters
instructor.bio: Born and raised on Liuqiu. Knows every reef on the island and is unreasonably good with first-timers and kids.

lanyu-5d4n.title: Lanyu 5D4N — Pelagic week
lanyu-5d4n.subtitle: Five days at the Kuroshio's richest edge. Mantas, dorado, whitetips — Lanyu in peak form.

day1.title: Day 1 — Arrival
day1.body:
Ferry from Taitung → arrive Kaiyuan Port → check in
Tank 1 — Bayue Bay (orientation)
Tank 2 — Maiden Rock

day2.title: Day 2 — Twin Lions & drift dives
day2.body:
Tank 3 — Twin Lions
Tank 4 — Mother Hen Rock (drift)
Night dive — Bayue Bay

day3.title: Day 3 — Blue Cave
day3.body:
Tank 5 — Blue Cave
Tank 6 — Maiden Rock deep
Afternoon culture tour

day4.title: Day 4 — Pelagic chase
day4.body:
Tank 7 — Yangjiao Bay outer reef
Tank 8 — Bayue Bay outer reef (mantas)
Tank 9 — Banquet Reef

audience.p1: Lanyu is for AOW-certified divers with drift experience. The Kuroshio rewards you, but it also asks for solid buoyancy and a cool head.
audience.p2: Your instructor adapts the order daily to the current and conditions. We want you to enjoy Lanyu — and we want safety to be the first dive every time.

instructor.name: Iris
instructor.creds: PADI IDC Staff Instructor · 12 years on Lanyu · pelagic photographer
instructor.bio: Lanyu's go-to pelagic guide. Knows the Kuroshio's rhythm and will walk you through Tao etiquette before you arrive.

aida.title: AIDA 2 Freediver
aida.subtitle: One breath. One ocean.
aida.intro: AIDA is the world's largest freediving certification system. Start with physiology and breathwork, then progress into apnea and depth — the most complete on-ramp to breath-hold diving.

theory.title: Day 1 — Theory
theory.body: Breath-hold physiology, breathing patterns, safety rules, manual study and quiz.

pool.title: Day 2 — Pool sessions
pool.body: Static (STA), dynamic (DYN), rescue procedures, equalisation drills.

open.title: Day 3 — Open water
open.body: CWT / FIM descents, target depth 16m+, safety in pairs.

cert.title: Certification
cert.body: AIDA international card issued within 30 days of completion.

price.note: Includes: instructor, gear, materials, certification, insurance, pool fees

e2.name: Low-volume mask
e2.note: Bring your own if you can

q1.q: Do I need to swim well?
q1.a: 200m continuous in any stroke is enough.

q2.q: I wear glasses — is that ok?
q2.a: Yes. Tell us your prescription at booking and we'll bring a corrective mask.

q3.q: Can I jump straight to AIDA 2 with no experience?
q3.a: Yes — AIDA 2 is the entry course.

q4.q: How deep will I be at the end?
q4.a: The standard is 16m. Most students hit 15–20m by graduation.

cta.title: Meet the ocean on one breath.

padi.title: PADI Open Water Diver
padi.subtitle: Your first scuba certification, globally recognised.
padi.intro: PADI is the world's largest scuba training organisation. The Open Water Diver course is your entry ticket — once certified, you can dive up to 18m anywhere in the world.

theory.title: Section 1 — Theory
theory.body: Dive physics, physiology, gear knowledge, buddy system, decompression concepts.

pool.title: Section 2 — Confined water
pool.body: 5 confined-water dives covering buoyancy, mask clearing, regulator skills, alternate-air sharing.

open.title: Section 3 — Open water 1–4
open.body: Four open-water dives applying skills in real conditions.

cert.title: Certification
cert.body: PADI e-card issued within 24 hours of completion.

price.note: Includes: instructor, gear, materials, certification, insurance

q1.q: Do I need to swim well?
q1.a: 200m continuous in any stroke is enough — no time limit.

q2.a: Yes — prescription masks available. Custom prescription recommended if you'll keep diving.

q3.q: I'm nervous about water — can I try first?
q3.a: Definitely. Book a try-dive first, decide later.

q4.a: PADI cards never expire. We do recommend at least one dive a year to stay sharp.

FAQ.title: Frequently asked
FAQ.subtitle: Courses, trips, gear, payment — the questions we hear the most. Can't find what you need? Hit us up on LINE.
FAQ.empty: We're still compiling this — ping us on LINE in the meantime.

q1.q: Can I take a course with zero diving experience?
q1.a: Yes. AIDA 2 (freediving) and PADI OWD (scuba) are both entry-level. They start with theory and confined water, then progress to the ocean step by step.

q2.q: Do I need to swim? How far?
q2.a: You'll need to swim 200 m continuously (freestyle or breaststroke, any pace) and tread water in the deep end for 10 minutes. If you're not there yet, spend a few weeks in the pool first.

q3.q: AIDA vs PADI — which should I pick?
q3.a:
AIDA is freediving — no tank, you hold your breath. It's about relaxation and body awareness.
PADI is scuba — you breathe from a tank, stay down longer, cover more ground.
Want 'the quiet of one breath' → AIDA. Want to 'wander reefs watching fish' → PADI.

q4.q: Can I dive with prescription glasses?
q4.a: Yes. We lend prescription masks (100–700 degrees / -1 to -7 diopters) — note your prescription on the form. For long-term diving, get a custom prescription mask for better fit.

q5.q: After getting certified, how deep can I dive?
q5.a:
AIDA 2 certifies to 16 m; most students hit 15–20 m by graduation.
PADI OWD lets you dive open water to 18 m.
Deeper requires advanced courses.

q1.q: Can I join a fun-dive trip without a certification?
q1.a: Fun dives require the matching cert (AIDA 2+ for freediving, OWD+ for scuba). No cert? Book a Discover Diving session or get certified first.

q2.a:
Usually included: boat/shore dives, tanks/weights, instructor/DM guiding, lodging, breakfast & lunch, marine insurance, site briefing.
Not included: personal gear rental, flights/ferries, dinner, drinks, tips.
Check each trip's 'included / not included' for specifics.

q3.a: We work with ferry operators to Ludao, Lanyu, and Liuqiu — we'll help you book tickets (at cost). Pickup from Taitung / Pingtung is available as an add-on.

q4.q: What if the weather's bad? Refunds?
q4.a:
Safety first. If weather/sea conditions prevent diving:
· Partial cancellation: substitute activity or refund the dive fee
· Full cancellation: refund minus committed costs, or roll over to the next batch

q5.a: Absolutely. Our trips run small-group (4–8 people) and solo sign-ups get paired with the group — many regulars first met this way.

q1.q: Do I need to buy my own gear?
q1.a:
Courses and most fun dives include the basics (BCD, regulator, tank, weights, 3 mm wetsuit).
We recommend bringing your own: mask, snorkel, fins, dive socks (hygiene + fit).
More experienced divers gradually build out a personal kit.

q2.a:
You'll fill in a health declaration at booking. Consult a dive physician before diving if you have:
· Cardiovascular disease, severe asthma
· Recurring ear/sinus infections
· Pregnancy
· Recent surgery / major illness
If you're sick or have allergies acting up — don't push it.

q3.a: All our trips include onboard accident insurance. We recommend students add DAN dive-specific insurance (covers chamber treatment). We'll walk you through it after certification.

q4.q: Can I fly right after diving?
q4.a:
No. To avoid decompression sickness:
· After a single dive: wait 12 hours minimum
· After multiple dives / multi-day diving: wait 18 hours minimum
Factor this into your travel schedule.

q1.a: Submit the booking form on any trip or course page. We'll confirm via LINE or email within 24 hours and walk you through payment.

q2.a: TWD bank transfer, LINE Pay, credit card (small processing fee via third-party gateway). International students can use Wise or PayPal.

q3.q: Deposit and balance — how does it work?
q3.a:
Courses: NT$ 3,000–5,000 deposit at booking, balance due 7 days before start.
Trips: pay in full or 50% deposit at booking, balance due 14 days before departure.

q4.a:
Before departure/start date:
· 15+ days out: full refund (less processing fee)
· 7–14 days: 70% refund
· 3–6 days: 50% refund
· Within 3 days / same day: no refund
Weather / force-majeure handled separately — see 'Trips' above.

cta.title: Still got questions?
cta.subline: Someone's on LINE pretty much all day. Or skip ahead — pick a trip and come meet the ocean.

about.title: About HeyHeyDive
about.body: Founded in 2018, HeyHeyDive specializes in freediving and scuba diving instruction, with bases in Taipei, Taichung, and Xiaoliuqiu. We help every student discover the world beneath the surface in a relaxed, joyful way. Beyond courses, we curate dive trips around the globe — once your skills are sharp, come join HeyHeyDive on an unforgettable underwater adventure.
